original_abstract: Graphics are widely used to provide summarization of complex data in scientific publications. Although there are many tools available for drawing graphics, their use is limited by programming skills, costs, and platform specificities. Here, we presented a freely accessible easy-to-use web server named SRplot that integrated more than a hundred of commonly used data visualization and graphing functions together. It can be run easily using all Web browsers and there are no strong requirements on the computing power of users’ machines. With a user-friendly graphical interface, users can simply paste the contents of the input file into the text box according to the defined file format. Modification operations can be easily performed, and graphs can be generated in real-time. The resulting graphs can be easily downloaded in bitmap (PNG or TIFF) or vector (PDF or SVG) format in publication quality. The website is updated promptly and continuously. Functions in SRplot have been improved, optimized and updated depend on feedback and suggestions from users. The graphs prepared with SRplot have been featured in more than five hundred peer-reviewed publications. The SRplot web server is now freely available at http://www.bioinformatics.com.cn/SRplot .
humanized_abstract: Graphics are often used to summarize large quantities of data that are contained within scientific publications. While there are many different applications that can be used to create graphics, the creation and use of graphics can be limited by such things as programming skills, costs to purchase and learn applications and by platform compatibility. In this study, we have developed a freely accessible online web server named SRplot that provides an interface to more than a hundred of the most commonly used data visualization and graphing functions. The web server can be easily used with all Web browsers and no special computing ability is required for the users’ machines. The SRplot web server has a graphical user-friendly interface (GUI) allowing users to simply copy and paste the data from their input file into the specific fields within the GUI provided in the web server, which can easily edit and plot the graphics on the fly. The plots created by SRplot can be easily exported to publication quality bitmap (PNG/TIFF) and vector graphics formats (PDF/SVG). The SRplot web server is a dynamic online application that is constantly being updated and expanded. The functions within SRplot are being continually improved, modified and optimized as well as being updated based on the valuable feedback and suggestions from users. To date, numerous plots have been created by SRplot and have been published in over 500 peer-reviewed scientific journals. The SRplot web server is available at the following URL: http://www.bioinformatics.com.cn/SRplot .
